vocab.example: Let's start the retrospective with what went well.
vocab.example_vi: Hãy bắt đầu buổi retrospective bằng những điều đã làm tốt.

vocab.example: The deployment went well this sprint.
vocab.example_vi: Việc triển khai đã diễn ra tốt đẹp trong sprint này.

vocab.example: We need to improve our testing process.
vocab.example_vi: Chúng ta cần cải thiện quy trình kiểm thử.

vocab.example: Let's write down the action items before we finish.
vocab.example_vi: Hãy ghi lại các việc cần làm trước khi kết thúc.

vocab.example: This sprint was busier than usual.
vocab.example_vi: Sprint này bận hơn bình thường.

vocab.example: Please be honest about what didn't work well.
vocab.example_vi: Hãy thẳng thắn về những điều chưa hiệu quả.

vocab.example: Communication between teams was a bit slow this sprint.
vocab.example_vi: Sự giao tiếp giữa các đội hơi chậm trong sprint này.

vocab.example: We should change our testing process next sprint.
vocab.example_vi: Chúng ta nên thay đổi quy trình kiểm thử trong sprint tới.

lines.speaker: Nam (team lead)
lines.text: Let's start the retrospective. What went well this sprint?

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: I think the deployment went well — no bugs after release.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: I agree. Also, the new pull request process helped a lot.

lines.speaker: Nam (team lead)
lines.text: Good. Now, what can we improve?

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: I think communication between teams was a bit slow this sprint.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: I agree. We should share updates more often, maybe daily.

lines.speaker: Nam (team lead)
lines.text: That's a good idea. Let's write it down as an action item.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Please be honest — I also think our testing process needs work.

lines.speaker: Nam (team lead)
lines.text: Noted. That's another action item for next sprint.

questions.q: What went well this sprint according to Hoa?
What will the team do about testing?
options: Make it an action item for next sprint

shadowing: Let's start the retrospective by talking about what went well this sprint.
Communication between teams was a bit slow this sprint, unfortunately.
We should share updates more often with each other, maybe every day.
Let's write it down as an action item before we finish today.
I also think our testing process needs some work before the next release.

interview.question: How does your team use retrospective meetings to improve?
tips_vi: Giải thích cấu trúc buổi retro: điều tốt, điều cần cải thiện, action item
Nhấn mạnh văn hoá trung thực, không đổ lỗi cá nhân
Nêu ví dụ một action item cụ thể đã thực hiện
interview.sample_answer: In my team, we have a retrospective meeting at the end of every sprint, usually for about thirty minutes. We talk about what went well and what we can improve, and everyone is encouraged to be honest and open, without blaming any single person for a mistake. After the discussion, we write down clear action items with a specific owner and a rough deadline for each one, so nothing gets forgotten. For example, once we noticed our testing process was quite slow and caused delays before every release, so we agreed together to add more automated tests. In the next sprint, we checked whether that action item was actually completed, which I think is an important step many teams forget. This process helps our team keep improving little by little every sprint, instead of repeating the same mistakes again and again.

interview.question: Tell me about a process improvement you suggested at work.
tips_vi: Dùng STAR: nêu vấn đề cụ thể, đề xuất, kết quả
Nhấn mạnh cách bạn thuyết phục team áp dụng thay đổi
Kết thúc bằng số liệu hoặc kết quả cụ thể nếu có
interview.sample_answer: During a retrospective meeting, I noticed that communication between our development team and the QA team was quite slow, which caused delays in fixing bugs before release. I suggested having a short daily sync, maybe just ten minutes, between the two teams instead of only sharing updates once a week. At first, some people were not sure it would really help, and one teammate worried it would waste time. Still, we agreed to try it as an experiment for two sprints and review the results afterward. After that trial period, our bug reports were handled much faster than before, and the whole team felt more connected and aware of each other's work. The QA lead even said the extra communication saved her team a lot of guessing. Now this daily sync is a normal part of our process, and we still use it today.
